articles.title: OpenAI’s next ChatGPT-5.6 upgrade may be too powerful to launch like a normal app update
articles.summary: The new ChatGPT-5.6 is releasing on Thursday in a limited preview and comes in three different versions.
articles.content: <p>Graham is the Senior Editor for AI at TechRadar. With over 25 years of experience in both online and print journalism, Graham has worked for various market-leading tech brands including Computeractive, PC Pro, iMore, MacFormat, Mac|Life, Maximum PC, and more. He specializes in reporting on everything to do with the most exciting subject in tech right now, Artificial Intelligence. AI is advancing at an accelerated pace and all the big brands from Apple, Microsoft and Google to chip makers NVIDIA are getting involved. TechRadar is here to bring you the latest updates on AI and show you how to get started and make it work for you, no matter your level of interest.</p><p> Graham has appeared on BBC TV shows like BBC One Breakfast and on Radio 4 commenting on the latest trends in tech. Graham has an honors degree in Computer Science and spends his spare time podcasting and blogging.</p>

articles.title: That free, two hour long Silent Hill game is getting a physical for some reason — and of course it costs $70

articles.content: <p>Dash is an experienced tech journalist who specializes in video games, electronic entertainment products, and the wider industry that surrounds them. He currently serves as the Gaming Editor at TechRadar, leading our review, preview, feature, and news coverage of the latest and greatest releases.</p><p>Before joining the team, he was Contributing Writer at PLAY (formerly Official PlayStation Magazine UK) and has written articles for many of the UK's other biggest gaming magazines including the likes of Edge, PC Gamer, and SFX.</p><p>Now, when he's not getting his greasy little mitts on the newest hardware or gaming gadget, he can be found listening to J-pop or feverishly devouring the latest Nintendo Switch otome.</p>

articles.title: News Corp Class B shares and the evolving media landscape
articles.summary: News Corp (NWS) Class B shares are positioned at the intersection of traditional publishing, real-time news, and digital real estate, providing investors with diversified exposure to the global media and information economy. The company's strategy involves shifting towards dig...
articles.content: News Corp (NWS) Class B shares are positioned at the intersection of traditional publishing, real-time news, and digital real estate, providing investors with diversified exposure to the global media and information economy. The company's strategy involves shifting towards digital formats and subscription models, alongside managing a portfolio that balances mature media assets with growth-oriented digital properties. This approach aims to stabilize earnings amidst industry changes, while its diverse revenue streams from advertising, subscriptions, and transactions are influenced by economic cycles and market-specific dynamics.

articles.title: United Rentals balances equipment demand and cyclical risks
articles.summary: United Rentals operates a significant equipment rental platform in North America, with its stock influenced by construction activity, industrial investment, and infrastructure spending. The company's diversified fleet and long-term themes like infrastructure renewal help mitig...
articles.content:
United Rentals Inc. (ISIN US9113631090) operates one of the largest equipment rental platforms in North America, giving it a central role in construction and industrial projects across the United States. The company’s stock is closely tied to trends in nonresidential construction, industrial capital spending and public infrastructure investment, making its business a bellwether for broader economic activity.

Scale and diversified rental portfolio

United Rentals Inc. has built a broad network of rental locations that serves contractors, industrial customers and public-sector entities across multiple regions. Its fleet spans general construction equipment, aerial work platforms, earthmoving machinery and a wide range of tools and specialty gear, allowing the company to participate in many different project types and end markets. This geographic and product diversification helps smooth out local or sector-specific fluctuations in demand.

The company’s rental model is capital intensive, with significant investment required to acquire, maintain and periodically refresh equipment. Over time, rental rates and utilization levels become critical levers for profitability, as even small changes in pricing or fleet usage can have a noticeable impact on margins. For investors, fleet age, utilization trends and pricing discipline are key indicators of how effectively management is balancing growth opportunities with return on capital.

Cyclical drivers and long-term themes

Because United Rentals Inc. serves construction and industrial customers, its revenue and earnings are influenced by business cycles. Periods of strong economic growth, higher industrial output and robust commercial construction typically support higher rental volumes and firmer pricing. In contrast, downturns can lead customers to defer projects, reduce spending or negotiate harder on rates, which can pressure utilization and margins.

At the same time, structural themes such as infrastructure renewal, energy transition-related projects and ongoing maintenance of existing facilities provide a backdrop of recurring demand. Analysts often frame the company’s outlook in terms of how these long-term drivers offset normal cyclical swings. For example, large multi-year infrastructure programs and industrial modernization initiatives can help stabilize activity even when private construction slows in certain segments.

Rental solutions and customer segments

One representative part of United Rentals Inc.’s business is its general rentals segment, which provides equipment and tools for construction and industrial projects of varying scale. Customers range from large national contractors handling complex commercial jobs to smaller local firms and tradespeople working on residential or light commercial sites. This mix allows the company to capture revenue from both big-ticket multi-month rentals and shorter, higher-turnover jobs.

In addition to core construction machinery, the company offers specialty rental solutions such as power and climate control equipment, trench safety systems, and portable storage. These categories tend to support industrial facilities, utilities, events and emergency response situations, providing incremental demand that is less directly tied to new-build construction cycles. For investors, the expanding role of specialty rentals is often viewed as a way to deepen customer relationships and diversify revenue streams.

Stock trading context

United Rentals Inc. is listed on a major US exchange and its shares trade in US dollars, giving the stock direct exposure to US equity market sentiment and to benchmarks that track industrial and construction-related companies. Daily trading activity reflects both company-specific expectations and broader views on economic growth, interest rates and capital spending.

articles.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities focuses on life science campuses as investors watch the sector
articles.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities specializes in developing and operating life science campuses within major innovation hubs, catering to the biotech, pharmaceutical, and research sectors. This strategy offers investors a specialized REIT profile with exposure to health care and...
articles.content:
Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ISIN US0152711022) is a real estate investment trust that specializes in owning, developing, and operating life science and technology campuses in key urban innovation hubs. The company targets locations with strong concentrations of biotech, pharmaceutical, and research institutions, positioning its portfolio to benefit from long-term demand for laboratory and office space tied to health care and scientific innovation. For investors, this focus provides exposure to a niche segment of commercial real estate linked to the growth of life science industries.

Life science campus strategy

The core strategy of Alexandria Real Estate Equities centers on clustered, campus-style developments designed specifically for life science tenants. These properties typically combine laboratory facilities, office space, and collaborative environments, supporting research and development activities across biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, diagnostics, and related fields. By concentrating assets in dense innovation districts, the company aims to create ecosystems where tenants can benefit from proximity to peers, academic institutions, and medical centers.

These campuses are structured to offer modern laboratory infrastructure, robust utility and safety systems, and flexible layouts that can be adapted as tenant research needs evolve. The focus on specialized building design differentiates the portfolio from more generic office REITs, as life science tenants often require higher capital investment in ventilation, power, and safety equipment. Over time, such requirements can support longer lease terms and tenant relationships, as relocating complex laboratory operations tends to be more challenging than moving standard office functions.

Positioning in the US market

Alexandria Real Estate Equities is part of the U.S. listed REIT universe and is associated with major innovation clusters such as Boston, San Francisco, San Diego, and other regions known for strong life science activity. By emphasizing markets with deep pools of biotech and research companies, the REIT seeks to align rental demand with ongoing investment in drug discovery, medical technologies, and advanced therapeutics. For U.S. investors, the company’s profile sits at the intersection of health care, technology, and commercial real estate, offering differentiated exposure compared with traditional office or retail properties.

Within the broader U.S. equity landscape, real estate investment trusts provide income-oriented characteristics through dividends and long-term leases, and Alexandria Real Estate Equities fits into this framework with a portfolio aimed at tenants engaged in research-intensive activities. Analysts often look at factors such as occupancy rates, leasing spreads, development pipelines, and tenant mix to assess the company’s performance. In the case of a specialized life science REIT, additional attention is paid to the stability of funding for biotechnology and pharmaceutical research, as well as broader health care policy trends that can influence tenant demand.

Representative properties and tenants

A representative example of the business model is a multi-building life science campus that hosts a mix of early-stage biotech firms, established pharmaceutical companies, and related service providers. Such campuses typically include laboratory buildings with advanced mechanical systems, adjacent office structures for clinical and administrative teams, and shared amenities such as conference centers, dining facilities, and green spaces. The goal is to create a cohesive environment where research, collaboration, and corporate operations can occur in close proximity.

Tenants in these properties commonly pursue drug discovery programs, clinical development, medical device innovation, and diagnostic technologies. Their work can involve partnerships with universities and teaching hospitals, making proximity to academic and medical institutions an important component of site selection. In many innovation clusters, Alexandria Real Estate Equities’ campuses are located near major research universities and health systems, reinforcing the ecosystem concept and supporting sustained demand for specialized space.

Stock and REIT characteristics

Alexandria Real Estate Equities trades as a U.S. real estate investment trust, giving investors access to a portfolio of life science and research-oriented properties through publicly listed shares. As with other REITs, performance is often evaluated through metrics such as funds from operations, net operating income, and dividend distributions, alongside traditional measures like occupancy and leverage. The company’s focus on laboratory and life science space can influence rental structures and capital expenditure requirements, which in turn play into long-term return profiles for shareholders.

articles.title: Bank of America Securities Reaffirms Their Sell Rating on Moderna (MRNA)
articles.summary: Bank of America Securities analyst Alec Stranahan reiterated a Sell rating on Moderna (MRNA) with a $38.00 price target, citing a 9.5% average return and 53.26% success rate for the analyst. This follows a similar Sell rating from J.P. Morgan and contrasts with a Hold rating f...
articles.content:
Bank of America Securities Reaffirms Their Sell Rating on Moderna (MRNA)

In a report released today, Alec Stranahan from Bank of America Securities reiterated a Sell rating on Moderna, with a price target of $38.00.

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

According to TipRanks, Stranahan is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 9.5% and a 53.26% success rate. Stranahan covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Moderna, Agios Pharma, and Revolution Medicines.

In addition to Bank of America Securities, Moderna also received a Sell from J.P. Morgan’s Jessica Fye in a report issued on June 18. However, on June 29, Barclays maintained a Hold rating on Moderna (NASDAQ: MRNA).

Based on Moderna’s latest earnings release for the quarter ending March 31, the company reported a quarterly revenue of $389 million and a GAAP net loss of $1.34 billion. In comparison, last year the company earned a revenue of $107 million and had a GAAP net loss of $971 million

Based on the recent corporate insider activity of 56 insiders, corporate insider sentiment is negative on the stock. This means that over the past quarter there has been an increase of insiders selling their shares of MRNA in relation to earlier this year. Most recently, in May 2026, Noubar Afeyan, a Director at MRNA sold 9,263.00 shares for a total of $433,878.92.

articles.title: Booking Holdings Inc. balances travel demand and digital scale
articles.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. navigates the travel industry by balancing digital demand, platform scale, and cost discipline. The company, which operates a multi-brand platform for accommodations, flights, and car rentals, focuses on technology, data-driven customer experiences, and d...
articles.content:
Booking Holdings Inc. (ISIN US09857L1089) is one of the largest global online travel companies, connecting millions of travelers with accommodation, flights, and rental cars across its portfolio of digital brands. The group, which trades on Nasdaq in the United States, combines mature businesses with ongoing investment in technology and marketing to defend its position in online travel.

Platform scale and global reach

Booking Holdings Inc. operates a multi-brand platform that spans hotel bookings, alternative accommodations, flights, car rentals, and travel-related services across many countries. Its flagship websites and mobile apps are designed to help travelers search, compare, and reserve stays ranging from large hotel chains to small independent properties, as well as apartments and vacation homes.

The company generates most of its revenue by charging commissions or fees on reservations made through its platforms. This asset-light model allows Booking Holdings Inc. to scale volume without the capital intensity associated with owning hotels or airlines. Over time, the company has expanded into complementary areas such as transportation services and travel insurance, aiming to capture a larger share of the total travel spend associated with each trip.

Travel demand and profitability focus

Travel demand is influenced by macroeconomic conditions, consumer confidence, and seasonal patterns, and Booking Holdings Inc. adjusts its spending on marketing and product development accordingly. The company closely monitors trends in international travel, domestic trips, and business travel as these segments can behave differently across economic cycles.

Profitability depends on maintaining an effective balance between marketing spend, technology investment, and operating costs. Analysts often pay attention to metrics such as room nights booked, take rates, and operating margin to gauge how the business is managing growth and profitability. Recent coverage has emphasized the importance of disciplined spending and efficient customer acquisition as competition from other online platforms and direct hotel bookings remains intense.

Digital brands and business model

Booking Holdings Inc. manages a portfolio of digital travel brands that focus on different parts of the travel journey. Some brands concentrate on accommodation reservations, others on flights or car rentals, and some provide meta-search and comparison tools. By running several brands under one corporate umbrella, the company can address varied customer preferences and regional needs while sharing data, technology, and marketing expertise.

The core business model is built on facilitating transactions between travelers and service providers. When a customer completes a reservation through one of the company’s platforms, Booking Holdings Inc. typically earns a commission or service fee from the hotel, apartment host, airline, or rental car company. In many cases, travelers pay the provider directly, while the platform takes its fee separately; in other cases, the company may collect payment from the traveler and then remit the net amount to the provider.

One strategic priority is to increase direct engagement through the company’s own apps and websites rather than via paid channels. Direct engagement can improve margins because it reduces reliance on paid search and other forms of online advertising. It also allows the company to build deeper customer relationships, support loyalty initiatives, and gather more data about travel behavior, which can be used to refine recommendations and pricing.

Alternative accommodations, such as apartments, homes, and unique properties, have become an increasingly important part of the offering. This segment broadens the addressable market beyond traditional hotels and can attract new types of travelers, including families and longer-stay guests. The company’s ability to present both hotels and alternative options side by side gives travelers flexibility while providing the platform with additional inventory.

Earnings cycle and investor perspective

Booking Holdings Inc. reports results on a regular earnings cycle, and investors often look at year-over-year trends in key metrics to gauge performance. Typical areas of focus include gross travel bookings, revenue, adjusted earnings, and free cash flow. These figures can indicate how effectively the company is converting travel demand into sustainable profitability.

Guidance and commentary from management about upcoming quarters can influence market expectations. When the company discusses investments in marketing, technology, and customer support, investors listen for signals about how aggressively it is pursuing growth versus protecting margins. Commentary about regional trends, such as demand in Europe, the Americas, or Asia-Pacific, can also be important because travel restrictions, currency movements, and competitive dynamics differ across regions.

In the broader context, online travel platforms are now a key part of how consumers plan trips. Many travelers start with digital searches to compare prices, read reviews, and check availability. Booking Holdings Inc. aims to capture this traffic through search engine optimization, app downloads, and partnerships with other companies. Over time, the company’s ability to convert online interest into confirmed bookings and repeat usage becomes a central determinant of its long-term value.

Technology, data, and customer experience

Technology and data are central to Booking Holdings Inc.’s strategy. The company invests in systems that can process large volumes of search queries, price points, and availability updates for hotels and other properties around the world. These systems must operate reliably at scale, particularly during high-demand periods such as peak holiday seasons.

Data analysis helps refine the user experience. By examining patterns in search behavior, bookings, and cancellations, the company can adjust how results are displayed, which filters are offered, and how recommendations are presented. For example, travelers might see options sorted by value, guest rating, or distance to city centers, depending on their preferences and prior activity.

Customer reviews play an important role in online travel. Booking Holdings Inc. provides a structure for travelers to leave ratings and comments about their stays. This user-generated content helps future customers make decisions and encourages providers to maintain quality standards. Over time, consistent reviews can reinforce the reputation of both properties and the platform itself.

Mobile is another key focus. Many travelers now search and book using smartphones and tablets, and Booking Holdings Inc. aims to ensure its apps and mobile websites are fast, intuitive, and secure. Mobile functionality, such as digital confirmations, maps, and customer support access, can improve the convenience of travel and reduce friction before and during trips.

Risk factors and competition

The business of Booking Holdings Inc. is exposed to several risk factors. Macroeconomic slowdowns can reduce discretionary travel spending, which may affect booking volumes. Changes in foreign exchange rates can influence reported financial results because the company operates across multiple currencies and regions. Regulatory changes affecting travel, payment processing, or online services can create additional compliance requirements.

Competition in online travel is intense. Travelers can book directly with hotels and airlines, use rival platforms, or rely on meta-search engines that aggregate offers from many sources. To remain competitive, Booking Holdings Inc. needs to maintain a compelling mix of inventory, pricing, and user experience. Marketing efficiency and brand recognition are also critical, as many travel decisions start with a search and familiarity with trusted platforms.

Another consideration is the relationship with supply partners. Hotels, alternative accommodation hosts, airlines, and car rental firms may negotiate commission rates and promotional placements on the platform. The company aims to offer a balanced proposition that delivers bookings to partners while maintaining attractive economics for its own business.

Representative product and service mix

A representative offering from Booking Holdings Inc. is its online hotel and alternative accommodation reservation service, which allows travelers to search a wide range of properties, compare prices, read guest reviews, and book stays in real time. This core service is designed to cover everything from budget options to premium hotels and unique accommodation types, making it a versatile tool for different budgets and travel styles.

Within this service, filters and sorting options help customers narrow down choices based on criteria such as price, rating, location, and amenities. Travelers can often see detailed descriptions, photos, and policies before confirming a booking. The platform aims to make the process straightforward while providing enough information to support confident decisions.

Stock and listing context

Booking Holdings Inc. is listed on Nasdaq in the United States, where many global technology and internet companies also trade. The listing provides access to a broad base of institutional and retail investors who follow technology-enabled consumer businesses. The stock reflects expectations about the company’s ability to capture long-term growth in online travel while navigating short-term fluctuations in demand and costs.

articles.title: Otis Worldwide focuses on long-term growth as an elevator and escalator leader
articles.summary: Otis Worldwide Corp. (ISIN US68902V1070) is a global leader in elevators, escalators, and moving walkways, known for its long legacy in vertical transportation technology. The company emphasizes a long-term growth strategy driven by a significant portion of its revenue coming...
articles.content:
Otis Worldwide Corp. (ISIN US68902V1070) is a major global manufacturer, installer and servicer of elevators, escalators and moving walkways, with a long legacy in vertical transportation technology.

Global footprint and service-driven model

The company generates revenue from both new equipment and a large installed base, with a significant share of its earnings coming from recurring maintenance and modernization services across commercial, residential and infrastructure projects.

Its operations span key regions including North America, Europe, Asia and emerging markets, where urbanization and high-rise construction continue to support demand for safe and efficient vertical transportation solutions.

Long-term strategy and innovation focus

Analysts often highlight the balance between Otis Worldwide's equipment sales and its service portfolio, which can provide relatively stable cash flows compared with more cyclical industrial businesses.

The company regularly emphasizes innovation in areas such as energy efficiency, digital connectivity, predictive maintenance and safety features, aiming to differentiate its offerings in a competitive global elevator and escalator market.

Representative product offering

One representative product line for the company is a modern elevator system designed for mid- to high-rise buildings, combining advanced control technology, compact machine-room or machine-room-less configurations and integrated safety features.

Otis Worldwide stock and listing

Otis Worldwide Corp. stock is listed in the United States, giving investors access to the company's shares via a major US exchange and exposure to its global elevator and escalator business.

For many market participants, the mix of new installations, modernization and service contracts is central to their view of the stock's long-term potential.

Summary fact box: Otis Worldwide Corp. is a global elevator and escalator specialist with a large installed base and recurring service revenue, listed in the US market and focused on innovation and safety in vertical transportation.

articles.title: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. $GS Shares Sold by Lmcg Investments LLC
articles.summary: Lmcg Investments LLC reduced its stake in The Goldman Sachs Group by 20.4% in Q1 2026, selling 8,505 shares and retaining 33,272 shares valued at $28.1 million. Despite this, Goldman Sachs reported strong Q1 results with EPS of $17.55 and revenue of $17.23 billion, surpassing...
articles.content:
Lmcg Investments LLC decreased its position in The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (NYSE:GS - Free Report) by 20.4% during the first quarter, according to the company in its most recent disclosure with the Securities & Exchange Commission. The firm owned 33,272 shares of the investment management company's stock after selling 8,505 shares during the quarter. The Goldman Sachs Group accounts for approximately 1.7% of Lmcg Investments LLC's portfolio, making the stock its 12th largest position. Lmcg Investments LLC's holdings in The Goldman Sachs Group were worth $28,148,000 at the end of the most recent reporting period.

Several other hedge funds and other institutional investors have also modified their holdings of GS. Norges Bank purchased a new stake in shares of The Goldman Sachs Group in the 4th quarter valued at $2,515,830,000. Corient Private Wealth LLC boosted its holdings in The Goldman Sachs Group by 1,657.7% during the 4th quarter. Corient Private Wealth LLC now owns 2,596,487 shares of the investment management company's stock valued at $2,282,312,000 after acquiring an additional 2,448,767 shares during the period. Wellington Management Group LLP grew its position in The Goldman Sachs Group by 110.8% in the 3rd quarter. Wellington Management Group LLP now owns 1,780,620 shares of the investment management company's stock valued at $1,417,997,000 after acquiring an additional 936,015 shares in the last quarter. Northwestern Mutual Wealth Management Co. grew its position in The Goldman Sachs Group by 428.4% in the 4th quarter. Northwestern Mutual Wealth Management Co. now owns 556,254 shares of the investment management company's stock valued at $488,947,000 after acquiring an additional 450,984 shares in the last quarter. Finally, Diamant Asset Management Inc. increased its stake in The Goldman Sachs Group by 84,499.0% during the first quarter. Diamant Asset Management Inc. now owns 422,995 shares of the investment management company's stock worth $35,785,000 after purchasing an additional 422,495 shares during the period. 71.21% of the stock is currently owned by hedge funds and other institutional investors.

The Goldman Sachs Group Stock Down 1.3%

Shares of NYSE:GS opened at $1,041.95 on Wednesday. The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. has a fifty-two week low of $691.30 and a fifty-two week high of $1,125.00. The firm's 50 day moving average price is $1,010.57 and its 200 day moving average price is $935.60. The company has a quick ratio of 0.63, a current ratio of 0.63 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 2.89. The stock has a market cap of $307.39 billion, a price-to-earnings ratio of 19.04, a price-to-earnings-growth ratio of 1.36 and a beta of 1.30.

The Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS - Get Free Report) last posted its quarterly earnings results on Monday, April 13th. The investment management company reported $17.55 EPS for the quarter, topping the consensus estimate of $15.92 by $1.63. The Goldman Sachs Group had a net margin of 14.13% and a return on equity of 16.53%. The company had revenue of $17.23 billion during the quarter, compared to analysts' expectations of $16.66 billion. During the same quarter in the previous year, the business posted $14.12 EPS. The firm's revenue was up 14.4% on a year-over-year basis. As a group, equities research analysts predict that The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. will post 60.18 earnings per share for the current fiscal year.

The Goldman Sachs Group Announces Dividend

The firm also recently announced a quarterly dividend, which was paid on Monday, June 29th. Investors of record on Monday, June 1st were issued a $4.50 dividend. This represents a $18.00 annualized dividend and a yield of 1.7%. The ex-dividend date of this dividend was Monday, June 1st. The Goldman Sachs Group's dividend payout ratio is 32.89%.

Insiders Place Their Bets

In other The Goldman Sachs Group news, insider Alex S. Golten sold 1,116 shares of the firm's stock in a transaction on Thursday, April 23rd. The stock was sold at an average price of $936.18, for a total value of $1,044,776.88. Following the sale, the insider directly owned 2,578 shares in the company, valued at $2,413,472.04. This trade represents a 30.21% decrease in their ownership of the stock. The transaction was disclosed in a filing with the Securities & Exchange Commission, which is available at this hyperlink. Also, CFO Denis P. Coleman sold 6,857 shares of the company's stock in a transaction dated Thursday, May 14th. The stock was sold at an average price of $973.55, for a total value of $6,675,632.35. Following the transaction, the chief financial officer directly owned 31,070 shares of the company's stock, valued at approximately $30,248,198.50. This represents a 18.08% decrease in their ownership of the stock. The SEC filing for this sale provides additional information. Insiders have sold 33,681 shares of company stock valued at $31,737,875 in the last three months. Corporate insiders own 0.55% of the company's stock.

Key Headlines Impacting The Goldman Sachs Group

Here are the key news stories impacting The Goldman Sachs Group this week:

  • Positive Sentiment: Bank of America raised its price target on Goldman Sachs to $1,150 and kept a buy rating, signaling confidence in upside from current levels.
  • Positive Sentiment: UBS also lifted its target on GS to $1,120, suggesting analysts still see room for the stock to appreciate.
  • Positive Sentiment: Multiple earnings previews pointed to growth in Goldman Sachs’ upcoming results, with analysts expecting the firm to benefit from stronger trading activity and dealmaking across Wall Street.
  • Positive Sentiment: Reuters reported that a surge in trading activity, helped by the SpaceX IPO, could lift second-quarter earnings at major Wall Street banks, including Goldman Sachs.
  • Neutral Sentiment: Goldman Sachs’ own strategists said the market’s “HALO trade” is entering a tougher phase where earnings performance matters more, which may be prompting a more cautious tone across equities.
  • Neutral Sentiment: Several articles highlighted Goldman Sachs research on other assets and companies, including bullish calls on Nvidia and Meta and a bearish take on gold; these reinforce the firm’s market influence but do not directly change GS fundamentals.
  • Negative Sentiment: GS has been cited in headlines about rising leverage in the market and hedge funds trimming riskier exposures, which can contribute to a more cautious trading backdrop for financial stocks.
  • Negative Sentiment: A Zacks note said Goldman Sachs dipped more than the broader market in the latest session, indicating near-term selling pressure and weaker relative performance.

Wall Street Analysts Forecast Growth

Several research analysts have recently commented on GS shares. Oppenheimer lowered shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from a "market perform" rating to an "underperform" rating in a research note on Tuesday, June 30th. Morgan Stanley raised their price target on shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from $1,021.00 to $1,099.00 and gave the company an "equal weight" rating in a research report on Monday, June 29th. Weiss Ratings upgraded shares of The Goldman Sachs Group from a "hold (c+)" rating to a "buy (b-)" rating in a research note on Tuesday, June 30th. BMO Capital Markets upped their price objective on The Goldman Sachs Group from $972.00 to $1,070.00 and gave the stock a "market perform" rating in a research report on Monday, June 29th. Finally, Rothschild & Co Redburn increased their price objective on The Goldman Sachs Group from $870.00 to $920.00 and gave the company a "neutral" rating in a research note on Thursday, June 25th. Ten equities research analysts have rated the stock with a Buy rating, thirteen have given a Hold rating and one has given a Sell rating to the company's stock. According to MarketBeat.com, the company currently has a consensus rating of "Hold" and a consensus target price of $986.91.

The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc is a global investment banking and financial services firm headquartered in New York City. Founded in 1869 as a commercial paper business, the company has grown into a diversified financial institution that provides a broad range of services to corporations, financial institutions, governments and individuals. The firm is led by Chief Executive Officer David M. Solomon and operates across major financial centers worldwide.

Goldman Sachs' core businesses include investment banking, global markets, asset and wealth management, and consumer banking.

articles.content: <p>Tom Bedford joined TechRadar in early 2019 as a staff writer, and left the team as deputy phones editor in late 2022 to work for entertainment site What To Watch. He continues to contribute on a freelance basis for several sections including phones, audio and fitness, as well as many other websites.</p><p>He graduated in American Literature and Creative Writing from the University of East Anglia. Prior to working on TechRadar, he freelanced in tech, gaming and entertainment, and also spent many years working as a mixologist.</p><p>He grew up in Bristol, UK, and has also lived in Norwich, UK, Salt Lake City, UT, and currently resides in London, UK. </p>

articles.title: Jail and caning for repeat drug offender who used friend’s NRIC to rent cars
articles.summary: Jackson Ong used a photocopy of his friend’s NRIC and driving licence to rent cars as he did not have a valid licence.
articles.content:
Jail and caning for repeat drug offender who used friend’s NRIC to rent cars

  • Jackson Ong caused a car accident in Ang Mo Kio and left a fake contact number, despite not having a valid driving licence.
  • He fraudulently used his friend’s NRIC to rent multiple cars and was caught with drugs and a knuckleduster.
  • Ong was sentenced to over four years’ jail, six cane strokes, an $800 fine, and a two-year driving ban for his offences.

AI generated

SINGAPORE – After getting into an accident at an open-air carpark, a man left a fake contact number but was identified by a witness.

Jackson Ong did not have a valid driving licence at the time of the offence.

He also committed several other offences, including drug consumption and using his friend’s NRIC to rent several cars.

For his various crimes, the 30-year-old was sentenced to four years, four months and two weeks’ jail, six strokes of the cane and an $800 fine after pleading guilty to nine charges on July 8.

The court heard that Ong had been driving home at about 8.10am on Nov 19, 2024.

When he reached the open-air carpark in Ang Mo Kio Avenue 10 where he lived, he collided with a stationary private ambulance while parking, causing it to collide with another stationary car.

Knowing that the vehicle owners were his neighbours, Ong left a fake contact number on their vehicles.

In August 2024, he also used a photocopy of his friend’s NRIC and driving licence to rent a Mazda 3 from a rental car company, as he did not have a licence.

The company repossessed the vehicle on Oct 10, 2024.

Investigations showed that Ong had used his friend’s details to rent two more cars from a different company in April 2025.

Deputy Public Prosecutor Joel Fun told the court that Ong’s friend received summons letters from the Housing Board and the Urban Redevelopment Authority regarding the rental cars on June 17, 2025.

His friend lodged a police report.

Drug and weapon possession charges

On March 19, 2025, Ong was arrested at his home. Officers who searched the premises found packets of methamphetamine and drug paraphernalia. Ong’s urine also tested positive for methamphetamine.

He was arrested again on June 24, 2025, in a rental flat, where officers once again found drugs on him.

They also found a knuckleduster in the unit. Ong admitted that he had bought it when he was in primary school. He said he knew it was an offence to keep the knuckleduster but had kept it because he liked it.

The DPP sought a jail term of between four years, four months and four weeks and four years, nine months and five weeks for Ong.

He also asked for six strokes of the cane, an $800 fine and a two-year driving disqualification to take effect upon Ong’s release from prison.

The DPP said that Ong’s offences involved the fraudulent use of his friend’s official documents.

Ong had also deceived the rental company into renting the cars to him.

He said: “The deception by the accused allowed him to obtain access to a motor vehicle despite lacking a valid driving licence.

“This created a high level of potential harm, which the accused did actualise by driving the vehicle without a valid driving licence or insurance, posing a danger to other road users.”

The prosecutor added that Ong was admitted to a drug rehabilitation centre in 2018 for consuming methamphetamine.

For driving without a valid licence, an offender can be fined up to $10,000, jailed for up to three years, or both.

For cheating by personation, an offender may be jailed for up to five years, fined, or both.

articles.title: 'No obligation to provide': Israel accused of withholding water from Jordan
articles.summary: 'No obligation to provide': Israel accused of withholding water from Jordan Despite a peace treaty between the two states that requires Israel to provide the Jordanians with water, officials have not signed a renewal agreement A view of the reservoir of the Mujib Dam, the main...
articles.content:
'No obligation to provide': Israel accused of withholding water from Jordan

Israel has refused to renew its water agreement with Jordan in violation of the peace treaty between the two countries, Israel's public broadcaster, Kan 11 reported on Monday.

According to the report, Israel has not yet signed on the renewal of the extended water supply agreement with Jordan, with pressure building on the Hashemite Kingdom due to its dependency on Israeli water.

"The issue of water is very important to us, and it is also part of the peace agreement," a Jordanian official told Kan 11, indicating his country may respond to the Israeli delay.

Amman, the report said, is afraid that Israel is using the water supply agreement as a bargaining chip in its relations with the kingdom.

In March, Kan 11 reported that Jordan's King Abdullah II conditioned a meeting with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on solving issues relating to water supply and the Palestinians.

The Israeli news site, Ynet also reported on the delay on Monday, claiming Israel is considering a proposal by the UAE to hold a trilateral summit in Abu Dhabi with the energy ministers of the UAE, Israel and Jordan.

According to Ynet, the suggested summit would address the water supply agreement as well as reviving the UAE-backed proposal for a joint Israeli-Jordanian desalinated water plant.

Abu Dhabi is trying to ease tensions between Jordan and its key ally Israel, which have increased after the Israel genocide in Gaza began in 2023.

"We continue supplying Jordan with the water required under the peace treaty," an Israeli official said,

The official added that Israel has "no obligation to provide additional quantities. If there is goodwill between the two countries, we can provide the extra water."

According to the Israeli official, "we wanted to fill our reservoirs for the benefit of our farmers," after what he described as the driest year in the last 100 years.

The Israeli official also told Ynet that "Jordan needs the water, but when you help your neighbours, you expect warmer relations," indicating that a possible summit in Abu Dhabi would deal with "normalisation, water and strengthening bilateral ties”.

Jordan dependency on Israeli water

Since the signing of the peace treaty between Jordan and Israel in 1994, water supply has become one of the most important issues between Israel and Jordan.

Water scarcity in the Hashemite Kingdom has increased over the years leading to Amman’s dependency on Israel's water supply.

In 1994, Israel agreed to provide Jordan with 50 million cubic meters of water annually as part of the agreement, with both countries committing to deal with water scarcity in the region by developing water sources, preventing pollution, and reducing water wastage.

In 2021, the Israeli government agreed to increase the supply of water to Jordan by an additional 50 million cubic meters of water, setting the water supply at 100 million cubic per year for a three-year period.

Since 2024, Netanyahu's government refused to extend the latest deal by more than six months, with Jordan requesting a five-year extension to the deal.

After he signed the latest extension of the deal in May 2024, Israel's Energy Minister Eli Cohen said that Israel "will continue to use our natural resources to strengthen Israel's standing in the region”.

In February, Jordanian outlet Al-Mamlaka TV reported that the kingdom had prepared measures aimed at securing the country's water supply regardless of political pressure or regional developments.

So far, Jordan is yet to publicly comment on the current water dispute with Israel.

Since the genocide in Gaza, relations between Jordan and Israel have significantly deteriorated, with Jordan formally recalling its ambassador to Israel, Ghassan Majali, in November 2023.

Last week, King Abdullah issued a royal decree officially ending Majali's mission in Israel and returning him to Amman with no replacement appointed.

While Israel is accused of using restriction of water supply to the Gaza Strip as a weapon of war, Kan 11 and Ynet reports indicate that Israel is also using water as part of its diplomatic efforts with a relatively friendly neighbour.

Jordan was part of the region-wide Arab effort to intercept Iranian missiles and drones targeting Israel during the 12-day war last year and the most recent conflict.

Global warming threat

As the effects of global warming expand worldwide, water supply is becoming a critical issue for countries around the world, and in the Middle East in particular.

A recent UN report said that "the planet has entered the Global Water Bankruptcy era," adding "nearly three-quarters of the world’s population lives in countries classified as water-insecure or critically water-insecure”.

According to Haaretz, Israel is a world leader in producing drinkable desalinated water, with 70 to 80 percent of the water supply to Israeli households coming from desalinated water plants across the country.

While Israel currently produces 804 million cubic meters of desalinated water per year, last month, the government approved a plan to raise Israel's drinkable water production capacity to 2 billion cubic meters per year by 2050.

articles.title: Show HN: Tarit – Self-host sandbox cloud and hypervisor for AI agents
articles.summary: We have built Tarit as a hypervisor built from ground up for running AI agent and RL environments. It is based on rust-vmm and can be used as a replacement for firecracker. Firecracker was built to serve a different need of primarily serverless compute and hence does not have...
articles.content:
We have built Tarit as a hypervisor built from ground up for running AI agent and RL environments. It is based on rust-vmm and can be used as a replacement for firecracker. Firecracker was built to serve a different need of primarily serverless compute and hence does not have primitives like live snapshots without pausing the VM operations.

We also provide a basic orchestrator that handles placement of the microVMs, creating clusters with HA, maintaining a warm pool of VMs, and takes care of setting up networking and monitoring.

Our benchmarks on a metal instance shows an acquire VM from pool and execute code p99 of 35ms which places it as one of the fastest VM based sandboxes you can get right now. You could also snapshot a small sandbox and resume in ~80ms.

You can easily self-host a multi-node cluster of this right now on your cloud with nested-virt enabled machines or better on bare metal by providing the docs to your coding agent.

Tarit - https://github.com/instavm/tarit

Quickstart - https://github.com/instavm/tarit/blob/main/orch/docs/QUICKST...
